patch.value: Decision: extension-point mechanism and bare-core fallback semantics for extracted audit flags (D1+D2 of pm-vjk3)

Maintainer decision gating S2/S3 of the audit-extraction phase-2 program (pm-vjk3). D1: how the plugin owns audit flags on core commands — (a) registerFlags(targetCommand) + registerCommand override for pm files/docs --audit handling, (b) package-owned subcommands (e.g. files-audit report under the package namespace), or (c) hybrid: registerFlags for allow-audit-* pass-through booleans + command override only where handler logic is needed. Also: add 'audit' alias to pm-governance-audit pm.aliases? D2: with the plugin absent, do non-owner audit-gated mutations fall back to --force only or hard-block; does the SDK retain a programmatic allowAuditBypass option (not a CLI flag); breaking-change posture for deleting runDedupeAudit/runCommentsAudit/runDedupeMerge/runNormalize from public SDK exports (first-party covered by same-cut lockstep + pm_min_version bump; external consumers lose the exports). Recommendation from filing pass: D1=(c) hybrid; D2=keep programmatic SDK option + --force fallback, document as breaking in the release notes item type.

D1 mechanism chosen and recorded with rationale; D2 bare-core fallback + SDK programmatic-option posture chosen and recorded; audit alias yes/no recorded; S2 (pm-tbd) and S3 (pm-tbd) unblocked or re-scoped per the decision

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: D1 mechanism chosen and recorded with rationale; D2 bare-core fallback + SDK programmatic-option posture chosen and recorded; audit alias yes/no recorded; S2 (pm-27mv) and S3 (pm-7dcf) unblocked or re-scoped per the decision

message: restore ACs with real slice ids

author: codex-pm-audit-extraction
message: Resolve D1/D2 before S2 and S3

patch.value:
## Decision

Choose the hybrid extension mechanism. `pm-governance-audit` owns the four audit commands and registers audit-only flags on existing core commands through `registerFlags`; package-owned handlers implement linked-artifact audit reports without keeping audit behavior in core. Add `audit` as a package alias so `pm install audit --project` resolves to the existing first-party package.

Bare core retains only generic ownership enforcement and accepts `--force` as the explicit fallback. No audit-named command, CLI flag, MCP parameter, completion, help entry, public SDK runner, or public SDK audit-bypass option remains in the default distribution. Internal generic enforcement values may remain only where they are not public audit API.

The four public audit runner exports are removed as an intentional breaking boundary change, coordinated with the first-party package minimum-version bump and changelog. This follows the user directive that the default CLI and SDK contain no audit functionality.

value.author: codex-pm-audit-extraction
value.text: Decision evidence: live extension contracts expose registerFlags and command overrides; the existing pm-governance-audit package is the canonical package-first target. Chosen hybrid ownership, audit alias, --force bare-core fallback, and zero public audit SDK surface.

patch.value: D1/D2 resolved: hybrid extension-owned commands/flags; audit alias; --force fallback; no public audit SDK surface.
One explicit mechanism, alias, fallback, and SDK-boundary decision guides implementation.
Hybrid registerFlags/package handlers, audit alias, --force fallback, and generic-only SDK primitives implemented and verified.
